objectives: Explain what a programming paradigm is and describe the procedural and low-level (assembly) approaches.
Apply the four addressing modes (immediate, direct, indirect, indexed) to determine the value loaded.
Define the key object-oriented concepts and apply inheritance and polymorphism to a scenario.

retrieval.q: What is the difference between a compiler and an interpreter?
retrieval.a: A compiler translates all at once into a standalone executable; an interpreter translates and executes line by line at run time (interleaving 1.2.2).

retrieval.q: Which methodology suits a project with unclear, changing requirements?
retrieval.a: Agile, because it works in iterative increments with continuous client feedback (interleaving 1.2.3).
A style or approach to writing programs, chosen to make a problem easier to write, maintain or reuse.

retrieval.q: What does the LMC instruction BRZ xx do?
retrieval.a: It branches to address xx only if the Accumulator equals zero.

retrieval.q: In immediate addressing, what does the operand hold?
retrieval.a: The actual value to be used.

keywords.def: A style or approach to writing programs, picked to suit a problem.
A paradigm using sequence, selection and iteration organised into procedures/subroutines, specifying how.
A low-level language using mnemonics, roughly one mnemonic per machine instruction, processor-specific.
The operand is the actual value to be used.
The operand is the address that holds the value.
The operand is the address of a location that holds the address of the value (a pointer).
A subclass acquiring the attributes and methods of a superclass, which it can extend or override.
The same method name behaving differently depending on the object, typically via overriding.

content.heading: Paradigms and procedural code
points: A programming paradigm is a style or approach to writing a program; no one language suits every task.
Pick the paradigm that makes a problem easier to write, maintain or reuse.
Procedural code uses sequence, selection and iteration organised into procedures/subroutines and specifies how (Python, C, Pascal).
It contrasts with object-oriented code, which models the problem as interacting objects.

content.heading: Assembly and the LMC
points: Assembly is low-level, using mnemonics such as ADD and STA, roughly one mnemonic per machine instruction and processor-specific.
It is used where speed, small size or direct hardware control matter, e.g. drivers and embedded systems.
The Little Man Computer is a simple model CPU and instruction set for learning assembly and the FDE cycle.
Key LMC instructions: INP, OUT, STA, LDA, ADD, SUB, BRA (jump), BRZ (branch if zero), BRP (branch if >= 0), DAT, HLT.

content.heading: Addressing modes
points: Immediate: the operand is the actual value.
Direct: the operand is the address holding the value.
Indirect: the operand is the address of a location holding the address of the value (used for pointers).
Indexed: the effective address is a base address plus the contents of an index register (used for arrays).

content.heading: Object-oriented concepts
points: A class is a template defining attributes and methods; an object is a specific instance created by instantiation.
An attribute is a variable (data/state) and a method is a subroutine (behaviour) of a class.
Encapsulation bundles data with its methods and hides the data (private attributes accessed via methods).
Inheritance lets a subclass acquire a superclass's members (DRY reuse); polymorphism lets the same method name behave differently per object.

worked_example.title: Addressing-mode calculation for LDA 6
steps: Given: address 6 holds the value 9, address 9 holds the value 4, and the index register holds 2.
Immediate: the operand IS the value, so the Accumulator is loaded with 6.
Direct: load the value held at address 6, which is 9.
Indirect: address 6 holds 9, so follow the pointer to address 9, whose value is 4.
Indexed: effective address = base 6 + index 2 = address 8, and the value is taken from address 8.
Key point: state which effective address each mode uses before reading its contents.

task: Given address 4 holds 7, address 7 holds 1 and the index register holds 3, state the value loaded by LDA 4 for immediate, direct and indirect modes, and give the effective address for indexed mode.
challenge: A program models Car, Bus and Motorbike, which share speed and a move() method but display themselves differently. Explain how a Vehicle superclass, inheritance and overriding a display() method (polymorphism) would be used, and give one maintenance benefit of this design.

exit_ticket.a: Class: a template defining attributes and methods; object: a specific instance of a class; method: a subroutine of a class defining its behaviour.

exit_ticket.q: For LDA 6 where address 6 holds 9 and address 9 holds 4, what is loaded in direct and in indirect mode?
exit_ticket.a: Direct loads 9 (the value at address 6); indirect loads 4 (address 6 points to address 9, whose value is 4).

exit_ticket.q: What is encapsulation and one benefit it provides?
exit_ticket.a: Bundling data with its methods and hiding the data via private attributes; it protects data from invalid changes and eases maintenance.
